sources: static/main.js::ui action handlers and data-env-action entrypoints

builder_motion.title: Builder Motion
builder_motion.description: Timeline, preset, authored clip, and scrub commands for builder-side motion work. These commands are easy to misunderstand if you assume preset apply means live playback.
when_to_use: Use these for staged motion inspection, authored clip work, or pose sequencing on the builder subject.
They are the core path for motion validation before live runtime playback is trustworthy.
mode_notes: Builder motion commands assume a builder subject and an active timeline or pose store.
Preset apply does not imply autoplay; staged motion and live playback are different surfaces.

builder_workbench.title: Builder Workbench
builder_workbench.description: Builder subject, scaffold, selection, and workbench editing commands for embodiment work in the character forge.
when_to_use: Use these when editing or inspecting the builder subject instead of the mounted imported asset.
This is the main family for scaffold truth, selection, pose editing, and blueprint state.
mode_notes: Most builder workbench commands assume builder_subject mode is active.
If builder mode is not active, expect partial responses or no visible effect until a builder subject is created.

camera_pose.title: Camera And Pose
camera_pose.description: Camera framing, orbit, pan, tilt, dolly, and reset controls for the theater view. Use these when you need repeatable framing instead of mouse-only inspection.
when_to_use: Use these before capture when you need repeatable framing instead of mouse navigation.
They are also the cleanest recovery path after focus or scene-shell changes leave the camera in a bad state.
mode_notes: Camera commands change viewer state, not embodiment or workbench data.
Framing commands depend on there being a meaningful focus, replay target, or overview context.

capture_observer.title: Capture And Observer
capture_observer.description: Observer capture commands used to corroborate live scene, motion, and embodiment truth instead of trusting the viewport alone.
when_to_use: Use these when viewport impression is not enough and you need saved evidence.
Capture commands are the main corroboration path for scale, floor truth, motion, and scene layout.
mode_notes: A dispatched capture is not proven complete until the file exists or shared_state reports the latest capture.
Different capture modes emphasize different truths: local detail, overview context, or motion across time.

character_runtime.title: Character Runtime
character_runtime.description: Mounted runtime commands for imported asset inspection, inhabitant presence, animation state, and clip control.
when_to_use: Use these to operate the mounted asset/runtime embodiment rather than the builder skeleton.
These are the commands for imported asset inspection, runtime animation, and inhabitant presence.
mode_notes: Some commands assume a mounted subject already exists.
Builder-subject mode and mounted-asset inspection can share the theater, but they are not the same runtime state.

environment_misc.title: Environment Misc
environment_misc.description: Cross-cutting environment controls that affect shell state, profile kits, world profile, or scene filters.
when_to_use: Use these for shell helpers, profile kits, world profile changes, and scene-side toggles that cut across families.
This family usually holds useful edge operations rather than the main command lanes.
mode_notes: Misc commands often affect shell state more than a single subject.
Read the individual entry carefully because the name alone is not always enough to infer scope.

focus_navigation.title: Focus And Navigation
focus_navigation.description: Focus, replay, and sample navigation controls that steer what the theater shell is currently centered on.
when_to_use: Use these when the theater focus, replay cursor, or live sample stream is pointed at the wrong thing.
These are the safest navigation controls when you want state changes without manual UI guessing.
mode_notes: Most focus targets only work when matching items exist in the current ledger, replay state, or scene context.
Expect shared_state focus keys and side panels to move together after a successful navigation change.

surface_bridge.title: Surface Bridge
surface_bridge.description: Owned-surface controls for opening, focusing, inspecting, and sending input into browser-side panels.
when_to_use: Use these when you need to drive a browser-owned panel without relying on a pointer.
They are especially useful for opening, focusing, and inspecting surfaces across agent sessions.
mode_notes: Surface bridge commands usually need a valid owned surface id first.
Success is easiest to confirm from visible browser behavior plus feed/shared_state.

theater_profile_recipe.title: Theater / Profile / Recipe
theater_profile_recipe.description: Top-level shell controls for theater mode, camera mode, replay mode, profiles, and recipes.
when_to_use: Use these when you are changing the shell itself rather than moving a single camera, subject, or panel.
These are the top-level steering controls for how the theater is organized right now.
mode_notes: A mode or profile change can invalidate assumptions about focus, camera, and visible inspectors.
These commands often affect multiple panels or shell affordances at once.

capture_probe.title: Capture Probe

target_contract.description: Optional target hint for the observer capture flow.

capture_probe.summary: Capture the multi-angle local probe view for the current subject or focused object.
when_to_use: Use this for close corroboration after timeline scrub, model swap, scaffold toggle, or load-field checks.
For mounted runtime validation, an explicit target hint like character_runtime::mounted_primary is the most reliable path.
what_it_changes: Writes new capture output and updates latest capture context.
mode_notes: In the current theater lane this is an env_control command, not a standalone /api/tool/capture_probe endpoint.
Treat the saved probe_*.jpg as the proof artifact; mirror fields help but the file is the durable corroboration.
gotchas: This is dispatched through the environment control lane; /api/tool/capture_probe is not a standalone tool in this build.
A blank target can still work when focus is stable, but explicit target ids are safer when the shell may have drifted.
failure_modes: Browser capture was queued but no saved output appeared yet.

capture_time_strip.title: Capture Time Strip

target_contract.description: Optional target hint for the observer capture flow.

capture_time_strip.summary: Capture sampled frames across the current builder timeline to inspect staged motion without live playback.
when_to_use: Use this after workbench_apply_motion_preset when you need evidence across the preset instead of a single scrubbed frame.
Use this for grounded motion validation because preset apply stages frame 0 and does not auto-play.
what_it_changes: Writes new capture output and updates latest capture context.
mode_notes: In the current theater lane this is an env_control command, not a standalone /api/tool/capture_time_strip endpoint.
The saved time_strip_*.jpg is the primary artifact. latest_time_strip_motion_summary in shared_state can lag behind the written file.
gotchas: This is dispatched through the environment control lane; /api/tool/capture_time_strip is not a standalone tool in this build.
The JPEG can be written before latest_time_strip_motion_summary settles in shared_state, so corroborate with the saved file when mirror fields lag.
failure_modes: Browser capture was queued but no saved output appeared yet.

character_set_model.title: Set Mounted Character Model

target_contract.description: Direct asset ref or URL for the mounted character model.

character_set_model.summary: Swap the mounted runtime to a direct asset ref and leave builder-subject mode for mesh-asset inspection.
when_to_use: Use this when you need to inspect an imported rigged asset instead of the builder subject.
For ground-interaction checks, prefer a footed character asset such as /static/assets/npc.glb or /static/assets/packs/kenney-blocky-characters/character-a.glb.
what_it_changes: Mounted runtime subject, animation state, playback, or inhabitant presence.
mode_notes: Some commands assume a mounted subject already exists.
Builder-subject mode and mounted-asset inspection can share the theater, but they are not the same runtime state.
Mounted-asset inspection and builder-subject mode are separate visual regimes.
gotchas: Uses direct asset refs/URLs, not manifest aliases.
Switching model while builder is active deactivates the builder subject.
Do not use non-footed novelty rigs like GhostArmature as proof of planted feet or grounded motion.
failure_modes: No mounted runtime character is active.
The asset ref could not be loaded or produced an empty mounted runtime.

mounted_asset_inspection.title: Mounted Asset Inspection
notes: Use this when validating imported assets outside builder-subject mode.
For ground-interaction validation, use a footed rig with a readable silhouette. npc.glb is the current default. GhostArmature is good for mounted-path plumbing, not planted-feet truth.
Corroborate floor planting with captures, not viewport impression alone.

motion_preset_validation.title: Motion Preset Validation
notes: Preset apply stages frame 0; it does not start live playback.
Use time-strip and timeline scrub together.
Current built-in preset ids are idle_shift, step_left, brace_crouch, and torso_twist.
capture_time_strip and capture_probe are env_control verbs in this environment lane, not standalone /api/tool/* endpoints.

grounded_motion_revalidation.title: Grounded Motion Re-validation
notes: idle_shift mid-scrub should stay double_support with both feet grounded at support_y 0.02.
step_left mid-scrub should show single_support_right with foot_r planted at support_y 0.02 and foot_l in swing.
brace_crouch mid-scrub should stay double_support with both feet planted while CoM lowers.
torso_twist mid-scrub should keep the lower body anchored in double support.
If the saved time_strip JPEG exists but latest_time_strip_motion_summary is empty, treat that as mirror lag first and corroborate with the file plus a second shared_state read.

builder_helper_strip_review.title: Builder Helper Strip Review
notes: Use this when validating what is browser-local versus what persists through runtime commands.
Turntable is local-only; scaffold and load helper toggles bridge into persistent commands.

builder_blueprint_roundtrip.title: Builder Blueprint Roundtrip
notes: Use this when verifying builder state as serialized data instead of relying on the viewport.
Capture after reload so the rehydrated builder state is corroborated visually.
